spec_match.rationale: The page does not load an actual HTML5 <video> or <audio> element. All states (:buffering, :seeking) are simulated manually via JavaScript click handlers toggling mock overlay classes, which fails to prove the actual browser platform implementation.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive mock. Features click-triggered simulation controls showing overlays, and mock indicator pills.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Correctly explains buffering and seeking concepts conceptually.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers stalled and seeking timeouts cleanly in JS code.

references.rationale: Includes a direct ChromeStatus entry reference link.

openQuestions.title: Replace JS mock with real video element
openQuestions.detail: Instead of manually mocking buffering/seeking transitions in JS, embed a real <video> element. Style it using real CSS media pseudo-classes (:buffering, :seeking) and use a slow video stream or large network buffer file so the browser's native media engine drives the selectors naturally.

summary: The buffering-states concept is a clean visual walkthrough but fails spec validation because it mocks all CSS states via JS rather than loading a real media element.
